title: BloombergNEF Turkey Transition Factbook 2026: Clean Power Growth Positions Turkey for the Next Phase of Its Energy Transition | BloombergNEF
gist: BloombergNEF's Turkey Transition Factbook 2026 finds Turkey on track to hit 120GW of renewable energy by 2035 but estimates nearly $1 trillion in cumulative power-sector investment is needed by 2050 for a net-zero pathway — roughly four times the current 2035 wind and solar target. Financing barriers from currency volatility and inflation remain the key bottleneck, even as Turkey emerges as the fourth-largest EV market in EMEA and prepares to host COP31.

insights.claim: Turkey has 34.2GW of solar and 15.8GW of wind installed as of mid-2026 and was a leading global market for solar and wind installations in 2025, according to BloombergNEF.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: Turkey's current installed base anchors the credibility of near-term targets and signals meaningful project-pipeline opportunities for equipment and EPC players.
insights.quote: The country was a leading market for solar and wind installations globally in 2025 and has 34.2 gigawatts (GW) of solar and 15.8GW of wind installed to date.

insights.claim: BloombergNEF expects Turkey to achieve its official target of 120GW of wind and solar capacity by 2035, driven by supportive policies, declining technology costs and growing electricity demand.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: BNEF endorsing the 120GW target as achievable de-risks long-horizon project financing conversations and validates Turkey as a priority deployment market.
insights.quote: BNEF expects Turkey to achieve its target of 120GW of wind and solar capacity by 2035, positioning renewables to play an increasingly central role in meeting rising electricity demand.

insights.claim: Reaching net-zero power in Turkey by 2050 would require nearly four times the country's 2035 wind and solar target, implying an installed wind and solar base well in excess of 450GW.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: The 2035 target represents only a fraction of the net-zero buildout; investors comfortable with long time horizons see a structural decades-long growth runway.
insights.quote: BNEF estimates that Turkey would need nearly four times its 2035 wind and solar target and close to $1 trillion of cumulative power-sector investment by 2050 to align with a net-zero pathway.

insights.claim: BloombergNEF estimates Turkey needs close to $1 trillion of cumulative power-sector investment by 2050 to align with a net-zero pathway, underscoring a massive financing gap beyond current commitment levels.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: A $1 trillion capital requirement over ~25 years signals sustained, large-scale infrastructure-financing opportunities for development banks, green bonds, and private infrastructure funds.
insights.quote: close to $1 trillion of cumulative power-sector investment by 2050 to align with a net-zero pathway

insights.claim: Currency volatility and elevated inflation in Turkey have complicated investment decisions and increased financing costs for clean-energy projects, making financing one of the most significant barriers to the energy transition.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: FX and inflation risk structurally inflate required returns; instruments like inflation-linked or USD-denominated off-take agreements may be necessary to attract institutional capital.
insights.quote: Currency volatility and elevated inflation have complicated investment decisions and increased financing costs for clean-energy projects.

insights.claim: Turkey plans to invest approximately $30 billion in transmission infrastructure between 2026 and 2035, requiring annual investment levels to increase almost fivefold from current levels.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: Grid investment is a prerequisite for renewable integration; transmission equipment manufacturers and engineering firms face a compressed, high-volume procurement cycle in Turkey.
insights.quote: Turkey plans to invest approximately $30 billion in transmission infrastructure between 2026 and 2035, requiring annual investment levels to increase almost fivefold from current levels.

insights.claim: BloombergNEF forecasts Turkey's grid-scale battery storage capacity to grow from near zero today to 8GW/24GWh by 2035, improving grid flexibility and reducing curtailment of wind and solar generation.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: An 8GW battery storage target from a near-zero base is a significant procurement signal for battery suppliers and storage project developers entering the Turkish market.
insights.quote: BloombergNEF forecasts battery storage capacity to grow from almost zero today to 8GW/24GWh by 2035, improving grid flexibility, supporting renewable integration and reducing curtailment of wind and solar generation.

themes.theme: Industrial Policy and Clean-Tech Supply Chains

insights.claim: Turkey is leveraging manufacturing subsidies, consumer incentives and local-content requirements to expand its domestic clean-energy industries and its role in global clean-technology supply chains.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: Local-content mandates create barriers for pure importers but reward companies that establish in-country manufacturing; monitor for partnership or JV requirements.
insights.quote: Manufacturing subsidies, consumer incentives and local-content requirements are supporting the development of domestic clean-energy industries.

insights.claim: Turkey is among the markets most exposed to the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), which is expected to influence trade dynamics for carbon-intensive exports.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: CBAM exposure creates urgency for Turkish industrial decarbonization; companies supplying decarbonization solutions (green hydrogen, CCS, energy efficiency) to Turkish heavy industry have a regulatory tailwind.
insights.quote: Turkey is among the markets most exposed to the European Union's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), which is expected to influence trade dynamics for carbon-intensive exports.

insights.claim: Turkey is launching a domestic carbon market in 2026 to support heavy-industry emissions cuts and maintain export competitiveness under the EU's CBAM framework.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: A new domestic carbon price creates a compliance cost that accelerates capex toward low-carbon processes; early movers in Turkish heavy industry gain a structural cost edge.
insights.quote: Turkey is accelerating heavy-industry decarbonization, with a domestic carbon market launching in 2026 expected to support emissions cuts and export competitiveness.

insights.claim: Turkey became the fourth-largest EV market in Europe, the Middle East and Africa in 2025, supported by growing manufacturing investment, tax incentives and purchase subsidies.
insights.speaker: BloombergNEF
insights.so_what: A top-four EMEA EV ranking validates Turkey as a priority market for OEMs and charging-infrastructure investors; incentive sustainability and FX risk remain key watch items.
insights.quote: The country's electric vehicle market has expanded rapidly, making Turkey the fourth-largest EV market in Europe, the Middle East and Africa in 2025.

insights.claim: Turkey's geography as a connector of trade between Europe, Asia and the Middle East, combined with its large industrial base, positions it as a strategic hub for clean-energy supply chains in a multi-polar world.
insights.speaker: Sofia Maia
insights.so_what: Strategic geography amplifies Turkey's appeal for clean-energy manufacturing FDI and cross-regional energy trade, reducing single-market demand risk for investors.
insights.quote: Turkey also benefits from geography as a connector of trade between Europe, Asia and the Middle East.

insights.claim: As host of COP31, Turkey has an opportunity to use the global climate summit to position itself as a model for how emerging markets can convert decarbonization into an economic growth strategy.
insights.speaker: Sofia Maia
insights.so_what: COP31 hosting typically accelerates domestic policy commitments and attracts concessional finance; Turkey's policy credibility window is near-term.
insights.quote: As host of COP31, Turkey has a chance to show how emerging markets can turn decarbonization into a growth strategy.
